questions.clue: Home to dozens of the world's largest technology companies and defined by its startup culture, this stretch of the San Francisco Bay Area took on its current character in the mid-20th century.

questions.clue: Carved into a South Dakota granite cliff between 1927 and 1941, this monument features the likenesses of four U.S. presidents chosen to represent the nation's founding, expansion, preservation, and development.

questions.clue: This prominent diplomat and humanitarian served as the United States' first delegate to the United Nations and played a leading role in drafting the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in 1948.

questions.clue: Born in Portland, Maine in 1947, this prolific author of horror and supernatural suspense fiction suffered a near-fatal collision with a van in 1999 while walking along a rural road near his home.

questions.clue: Glaciologists keep drill core samples of this frozen substance — key evidence of ancient climate records — sealed in temperature-controlled containers in the field office.
